_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 13: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and relevant ISO standards. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 13 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Recital 72 (legislative intent on transparency)
ISO/IEC TR 24028:2020 — Overview of trustworthiness in artificial intelligence
ISO/IEC TS 6254:2024 — Transparency taxonomy of AI systems
ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020 — Testing of AI-based systems
NIST AI 100-1 (AI Risk Management Framework — Transparency)
EU High-Level Expert Group on AI — Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 13 (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Instructions of Use for Deployers
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST provide instructions of use in an appropriate digital format accompanying the high-risk AI system.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(3) mandates that instructions of use accompany the system. These instructions enable deployers to fulfill their own obligations under Art. 26.
required_content: Provider identity, name, and contact information
Intended purpose of the AI system
Level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity metrics
Known or foreseeable circumstances creating risks
Performance across different groups of persons (where applicable)
Expected lifetime of the system and maintenance needs
acceptable_formats: Deployer guide (digital document: MD, PDF, HTML)
User manual with dedicated AI transparency section
Structured data file (JSON/YAML) alongside human-readable guide

requirements.title: Model Interpretability
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD integrate interpretability mechanisms enabling deployers to understand the basis for individual predictions.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(1) requires the system to be 'sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret the system's output and use it appropriately.' Interpretability tools are the standard mechanism for achieving this.
acceptable_implementations: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) — feature importance per prediction
Decision tree extraction or rule-based surrogate models
requirements.note: The level of interpretability needed depends on the risk level and domain. Medical/legal AI requires deeper interpretability than recommendation systems.

requirements.title: Output Confidence / Uncertainty Scores
requirements.requirement: The AI system SHOULD expose calibrated confidence or uncertainty scores alongside predictions to enable deployers to appropriately calibrate trust.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(3)(b)(ii) requires disclosure of 'the level of accuracy, including its metrics.' Per-prediction confidence scores enable deployers to set human review thresholds and identify cases requiring additional scrutiny.
acceptable_implementations: Calibrated prediction probabilities (e.g., Platt scaling, isotonic regression)
Softmax scores with documented calibration methodology

requirements.title: Intended Purpose Documentation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST document the intended purpose of the AI system, including use cases and foreseeable misuse.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(3)(b)(i) requires instructions to include 'the intended purpose of the AI system.' This is foundational — deployers cannot use a system appropriately if they do not understand its intended purpose.
required_content: Decision types supported by the system

requirements.title: Known Limitations Documentation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST document known limitations, foreseeable risks, and circumstances under which the system may underperform.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(3)(b)(iv) requires documenting 'the known or foreseeable circumstances, related to the use of the high-risk AI system in accordance with its intended purpose or under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse, in which the AI system may create risks to the health, safety or fundamental rights.'
required_content: Known failure modes and edge cases
Input types that cause degraded performance
Conditions under which the system should not be used
Foreseeable risks from reasonably foreseeable misuse

requirements.title: Input Data Specifications
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST document input data specifications, including expected formats, valid ranges, and quality requirements.
requirements.rationale: Art. 13(3)(b)(iii) requires 'the specifications for the input data, or any other relevant information in terms of the training, validation and testing datasets used.'
required_content: Known input types that cause errors

scoring.compliant: R01 + R04 + R05 met, and at least 2 of R02, R03, R06 met
scoring.partial: R01 or R04 met + at least 2 others met
